# @andes/context

Shared utilities to consume/provide internal and external application context.

## How to use

### Installation

> Follow the [general installation guide](../../docs/installation.md).

```bash
npm install --save @andes/context
```

### Nordic applications

By default, from version `8.0.0`, every Andes component requires the usage of `@andes/context` both server and client side in order to improve usability and accessibility.
If you are using Nordic pages, this feature is already included by default on your page scaffolding and you don't have to do anything else.
Otherwise, make sure to wrap your application with an `<AndesProvider>` tag as following:

```jsx
// app/pages/my-page/controller.js
import { AndesProvider } from '@andes/context';
import View from './view';

export function render(req, res) {
  const { device, i18n, locale } = req;
  const _locale = locale || i18n.locale;

  const MyPage = (props) => (
    <AndesProvider locale={_locale} device={device}>
      <View {...props} />
    </AndesProvider>
  );

  res.render(Demo, { device, locale: _locale });
};

...
// app/pages/my-page/view.js
import React from 'react';
import Page from 'nordic/page';

class View extends React.Component {
  render() {
    const { locale, device } = this.props;

    return(
      <Page
        name="myPage"
        className="my-page"
        state={{ locale, device }}
      >
        {/* Your page content */}
      </Page>
    )
  }
}

export default View;

...

// app/client/my-page.js
import React from 'react';
import { AndesProvider } from '@andes/context';
import { hydrate } from 'nordic/hydrate';
import MyPage from '../pages/my-page/view';

const initialState = window.__PRELOADED_STATE__;

hydrate(
  <AndesProvider locale={initialState.locale} device={initialState.device}>
    <MyPage {...initialState} />
  </AndesProvider>,
  document.getElementById('root-app'),
);
```

### `AndesProvider` API

| Property | Description | Type | Default | Required |
| -------- | ----------- | ---- | ------- | -------- |
| `locale` | <sup>1</sup>Application locale | `oneof(LOCALES_LIST)` | `-` | ✅ |
| `children` | Single React element | `element` | `-` | ✅ |
| `csrfToken` | <sup>2</sup>CSRF protection token | `string` | `undefined` |  |
| `device` | <sup>3</sup>Object with device information | `object` | `undefined` |  |

> 1 - Available locales:

```jsx
LOCALES_LIST = [
  'es-AR',
  'pt-BR',
  'es-PE',
  'es-BO',
  'es-CL',
  'es-CO',
  'es-CR',
  'es-DO',
  'es-EC',
  'es-GT',
  'es-HN',
  'es-NI',
  'es-PA',
  'es-PR',
  'es-PY',
  'es-SV',
  'es-UY',
  'es-VE',
  'es-MX',
  'en',
  'en-US',
  'zh-CN'
]
```

> 2 - This token is used inside the `Form` component

> 3 - This object is used to get device information. For more information read [device](https://github.com/melisource/fury_frontend-device-detection#reqdevice)

### Internal components usage

#### Common scaffold

```json
// translations/es-AR/messages.json
{
  "andes-{name}\u0004mensaje de prueba": [null, "mensaje de prueba"]
}
```

```json
// translations/pt-BR/messages.json
{
  "andes-{name}\u0004mensaje de prueba": [null, "mensagem de teste"]
}
```

```json
// translations/en/messages.json
{
  "andes-{name}\u0004mensaje de prueba": [null, "test message"]
}
```

#### Consuming context in functional components

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { useAndesContext } from '@andes/context';
import enTranslations from './translations/en/messages.json';
import esTranslations from './translations/es-AR/messages.json';
import ptTranslations from './translations/pt-BR/messages.json';

const Component = ({ children }) => {
  ...
  const i18n = useAndesContext('test-component', { en: enTranslations, 'es-AR': esTranslations, 'pt-BR': ptTranslations });
  ...

  return (
    <div className="my-component" aria-label={i18n.pgettext('mensaje de prueba')}>
      {children}
    </div>
  );
}

export default Component;
```

#### Consuming context in class components

Wrap your component in a Container component and pass the `i18n` prop:

```jsx
import React from 'react';
import { useAndesContext } from '@andes/context';
import enTranslations from './translations/en/messages.json';
import esTranslations from './translations/es-AR/messages.json';
import ptTranslations from './translations/pt-BR/messages.json';

const ComponentContainer = props => {
  const i18n = useAndesContext('test-component', { en: enTranslations, 'es-AR': esTranslations, 'pt-BR': ptTranslations });

  return (
    <Component i18n={i18n} {...props} />
  );
}
```

```jsx
import React from 'react';

class Component extends React.Component {
  render() {
    const { children, i18n } = this.props;

    return (
      <div className="my-component" aria-label={i18n.gettext('mensaje de prueba')}>
        { children }
      </div>
    );
  }
}

export default Component;
```
